title: At Long Last Leave

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer's iconic frustrated exclamation opens the episode
jokes.explanation: Homer's signature catchphrase - purely functional but iconic to the character

jokes.text: Why, you little...!
jokes.punchline: The incomplete threat that never gets finished
jokes.explanation: Another Homer signature line - familiar comfort food comedy

jokes.text: Good evening. It is with great sadness I inform you that America and China have declared war, and a massive nuclear attack is expected to reach our shores within the hour. That's the sort of hypothetical emergency today's disaster preparedness drill is designed to get us ready for.
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's just a drill scenario
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that plays on audience expectations and current events fears

jokes.text: Sorry, Dad. I was afraid the dragon wouldn't cough the moon back up.
jokes.setup: Bart apologizing for panicking during eclipse
jokes.punchline: His belief that a dragon swallows the moon
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdist mythology that fits Bart's character while satirizing superstition

jokes.text: You idjit! The dragon always coughs the moon back up!
jokes.punchline: His confident assertion about dragon behavior
jokes.explanation: Homer's supreme confidence in completely false information is peak character comedy

jokes.text: Then why am I paying $800 a year in dragon insurance?
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that Homer has monetized his delusion - perfect economic absurdity

jokes.text: Lisa, everyone knows dragons do not attack cars. Geez, pick up a book.
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - he's confidently wrong but treats Lisa like she's ignorant

jokes.text: I pick up books like you pick up beers!
jokes.explanation: Solid comparison joke that reveals character dynamics

jokes.text: Then you have a serious reading problem.
jokes.setup: Lisa comparing her reading to his drinking
jokes.punchline: Homer misses the point about his drinking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obtuseness - he completely misses Lisa's point about his alcoholism

jokes.text: Is what I would say, if this weren't a drill. This is not a drill!
jokes.explanation: Decent misdirection but predictable given Homer's character

jokes.text: So do your farting now.
jokes.setup: Family going into shelter for three hours
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer crudeness - character appropriate but lowbrow

jokes.text: Don't worry. My last words will be 'I can outrun that lion.'
jokes.setup: Marge worried about his last words
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - perfectly captures Homer's misplaced confidence

jokes.text: All right, who's the wisenheimer that's been eating the powdered food? Not me. Two can play at that game.
jokes.punchline: Homer denies it while actively eating more
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer hypocrisy - denies while immediately doing the thing

jokes.text: Boy, I told you an hour ago, stop that! But Bunkerball is keeping us sane!
jokes.setup: Homer telling Bart to stop something
jokes.explanation: Good invented word and concept - fits Bart's creative mischief

jokes.text: I could sneak into the school and write whatever I want on the chalkboard!
jokes.setup: Family discussing what to do if town is empty
jokes.punchline: Bart's reference to the show's opening gag
jokes.explanation: Nice meta-reference to the show's opening chalkboard gag

jokes.text: I could see a planetarium show without the secondhand marijuana!
jokes.punchline: Implied drug use at planetarium shows
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor about planetarium culture from innocent Lisa

jokes.text: And I can drive drunk while I'm actually sober! It's fun, but not as fun.
jokes.explanation: Dark admission about Homer's regular drunk driving - character consistent

jokes.text: Oh, honey, why must you always assume that a huge picture of us at a secret meeting we weren't told about is a bad thing?
jokes.setup: Family seeing their picture at secret town meeting
jokes.punchline: Marge's sarcastic optimism about obvious bad situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge deadpan - her eternal optimism meets undeniable bad news

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.explanation: Callback to opening - appropriate use of signature catchphrase

jokes.text: They're here! The monsters are here! Moe, it's me, Marge. I'm your friend. The monster queen is coming on to me!
jokes.punchline: He thinks friendly Marge is seducing him
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - Moe's paranoia and misinterpretation of Marge's kindness

jokes.text: I'll have you know I'm also a hair donor! Salma Hayek wore me to the Oscars!
jokes.punchline: His hair was used by celebrity
jokes.explanation: Absurd character detail - random background character has celebrity connection

jokes.text: Even baby never cry! She's a freak! What baby does that?!
jokes.punchline: Baby Maggie being too well-behaved is somehow bad
jokes.explanation: Good ironic complaint - even positive baby behavior is twisted into negative

jokes.text: We won! Whoo-hoo!
jokes.setup: Being told they're bankrupting the city
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer missing the point - celebrates destructive behavior

jokes.text: Homer, that is not banishment-hearing behavior!
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge response - treating banishment like social etiquette

jokes.text: Marge, you are a sweet woman and you make us see your family in a more forgiving light. Thank you. Which is why you're the worst Simpson of all!
jokes.punchline: Her goodness makes her the worst family member
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - builds up Marge only to tear her down with twisted logic

jokes.text: but, uh, I believe it was Sammy Hagar who said... Silence! D'oh! That was an 'inside me' bone!
jokes.setup: Homer trying to quote Sammy Hagar
jokes.punchline: Gets hit and makes up bone anatomy
jokes.explanation: Decent physical comedy with Homer's made-up anatomy

jokes.text: I withdraw my... diddily.
jokes.punchline: His defeat expressed in characteristic speech pattern
jokes.explanation: Perfect Flanders deflation - his characteristic speech tic shows defeat

jokes.text: Oh... I always thought 'tarred and feathered' was just a figure of speech.
jokes.setup: Homer being literally tarred and feathered
jokes.explanation: Decent literalism humor - Homer's naive surprise at actual consequences

jokes.text: Good luck patching potholes and stuffing pillows now!
jokes.punchline: His assumption about what tar and feathers are used for
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic - he thinks they wasted useful municipal supplies

jokes.text: Hey, Henrietta Hippo, I've got to drain the inchworm.
jokes.punchline: His crude euphemisms for Marge and urination
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer crudeness - character appropriate but lowbrow humor

jokes.text: Should I get my helmet? Helmet? That's Springfield talk.
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between safety-conscious city life and dangerous frontier culture

jokes.text: Is there gas in these? Springfield talk!
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of 'Springfield talk' running gag

jokes.text: I'm trying to make the best of things, but I'm worried Maggie is falling in with a bad crowd.
jokes.punchline: Implication that other babies are 'bad influences'
jokes.explanation: Good parental anxiety humor applied absurdly to baby social dynamics

jokes.text: How're you doin', Mr. Assange? That's my personal information, and you have no right to know about it.
jokes.setup: Homer's friendly greeting to WikiLeaks founder
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of Assange's radical transparency vs. personal privacy contradiction

jokes.text: Is it Iraqi journalists being murdered? Don't be ridiculous. It's an Afghan wedding being bombed.
jokes.explanation: Dark political satire about WikiLeaks content as casual entertainment

jokes.text: Well I've got a really big secret for you. I'm not wearing any underwear. You know, you should really get out less.
jokes.explanation: Decent character clash - Homer's oversharing meets Assange's selectivity

jokes.text: Homie, these disguises won't fool anyone. Pish tosh, Smithers, and fiddle-dee-dee! Heh-heh, nailed it.
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks he's perfectly disguised as Mr. Burns
jokes.explanation: Good Homer delusion - his terrible Mr. Burns impression

jokes.text: Excellent! Yeah, it wouldn't be you if you didn't say 'excellent.'
jokes.explanation: Decent meta-humor about character catchphrases

jokes.text: You know what's great about you, Marge? After all these years, you can still shinny up the donut and then pull me up after you.
jokes.setup: Homer being romantic at Lard Lad donut
jokes.punchline: His romantic gesture involves climbing a giant donut
jokes.explanation: Sweet Homer romance mixed with absurd Springfield imagery

jokes.text: You ever wonder if there are donut shops on other planets? On a night like tonight, I have to believe there are.
jokes.explanation: Beautiful Homer moment - genuine romance filtered through his donut obsession

jokes.text: Eh, there's no sex like fugitive sex.
jokes.setup: Homer and Marge in their old bedroom
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer crudeness - mildly funny but lowbrow

jokes.text: This place is awesome, but it's not puppy-safe. And my little guy's coming home tomorrow.
jokes.explanation: Good character subversion - hardened criminal has soft spot for puppy

jokes.text: Shh. I think I heard a pair of underpants being picked up off the ground. Big ones.
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Wiggum absurdity - impossibly specific detection of underwear sounds

jokes.text: I'm not the sharpest pencil in the... pencil thing, but I'm least as smart as a cat. Right, Lou? Uh, what breed, Chief? I mean, I saw an Abyssinian once who could change channels.
jokes.punchline: The comparison gets more specific and undermining
jokes.explanation: Great escalating Wiggum incompetence - he talks himself into worse comparisons

jokes.text: Crucify them! And screw the boulder in tight! Make them write monologue jokes! Cancel their subscription to the opera! We hate opera! Then get them a subscription to the opera!
jokes.punchline: Escalating from crucifixion to forcing opera subscription
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation from violent to petty to contradictory punishments

jokes.text: Maybe he's right-- we are jerks. You want I should spray some of my Jerk Off on you?
jokes.punchline: Moe's accidental sexual innuendo with cleaning product
jokes.explanation: Decent innuendo humor - Moe's innocent offer sounds inappropriate

jokes.text: This is the outlands, boy. Ideas aren't good or bad, they're just free. I was wrong-- there are bad ideas! There are terrible ideas!
jokes.explanation: Great philosophical hypocrisy - libertarian ideals meet harsh reality

jokes.text: Now, I got to warn you, if you find a little black thing on your pillow, it won't be a mint.
jokes.punchline: Subverts hotel mint expectation with something sinister
jokes.explanation: Sharp subversion of hospitality expectations with frontier dangers

jokes.text: Let her slice off the tip of your ear, and she'll go right to sleep. No. That's not a choice you get to make.
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about frontier survival vs. personal choice

jokes.text: I, uh, heard everything was going great in your new Eden, with one flaw: you lack a corrupt city government. True, true. And a school system that teaches to the tests, sneaks the kids the answers and still fails.
jokes.punchline: Their selling points are corrupt systems
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire of institutional dysfunction presented as benefits

jokes.text: Humanity is an inexorable upward march! Or was till the year 2000.
jokes.explanation: Sharp political commentary about civilizational decline in 21st century

jokes.text: Ow! Accident. Here comes another one. Ow!
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy - repetitive but functional ending beat

tmdb_overview: When the Simpsons are kicked out of Springfield, they join an off-the-grid community near by; Homer and Marge's former friends react badly when they try to sneak back into town.
